steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The passage opens with Unlike Kuroda Seiki, who adopted traditional European methods. This contrast signals that Hishida took the opposite approach with Japanese painting.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Embraced means to accept and actively support—exactly what the example shows Hishida did with traditional silk and pigments. The other three choices (distrusted, released, overlooked) all imply rejection or inattention rather than engagement. That matches choice B — B is correct.
